title: Tool Artifact
description: One tool the LLM MAY call. Wave 2.5-A ships the catalog + schema + registry; Wave 2.5-B adds the ToolExecutor and cross-check function-calling integration. See docs/roadmap/prompt-composition.md § Tool use subsystem.

id.description: Stable identifier. Version is a separate field so id stays stable across versions.

description.description: The one-line description shown to the model. Kept short so the tool manifest stays cache-friendly and does not trigger the 'lost in the middle' failure mode.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the tool call arguments. The executor validates every call against this schema BEFORE dispatching to the provider.

capability_gate.description: When the tool is eligible to be offered to the model. Composer filters tools by these gates before adding them to a role's tool manifest.

requires_novelty_score.description: Comparison expression like '>= 0.7'. The composer parses this string; unrecognized syntax is a fail-fast defect.

allowlist.description: Provider-specific allowlist. For web.search this holds the allowed domain suffixes; for local tools it MAY be absent.

output_wrapper.description: The XML tag pattern used to inject tool output into the next model turn. MUST include trusted="false" so the model treats the content as data. Example: <tool_result trusted="false" tool="rule.query">...</tool_result>.

default_mode.description: Shadow-vs-enforce mode declared by the tool. A shadow tool is loaded and listed in the manifest for probes but never actually dispatched to its provider until promoted.

provider.description: DI seam identifier that the ToolExecutor resolves to a concrete provider (e.g. 'RuleCatalogQueryProvider'). Optional in Wave 2.5-A so tool YAMLs can be authored before the executor lands.
